zh.lifeSummary: 當Gwenllian Elizabeth Sibil Delahue de la Bere於大約1402年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根爾時，她的父親John de la Bere IV19歲，母親Joanna Barre30歲。她與Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1450年在英國英格蘭享年49歲。

zh-hans.Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Ki: 英国威尔斯蒙姆爾紐波特
zh-hans.Glamsorgan, Wales, United Kingdom: 英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gwenllian Elizabeth Sibil Delahue de la Bere于大约1402年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根爾时，她的父亲John de la Bere IV19岁，母亲Joanna Barre30岁。她与Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1450年在英国英格兰享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Bere
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Beer  .

variants.name: Beer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erhard, Hans, Heinz, Aloys, Bodo, Erna, Ernst, Frieda, Friedhelm, Hanni.

English (West Country): habitational name from any of the forty or so places in southwestern England called Beer(e) or Bear(e). Most of these derive their names from the West Saxon dative case, beara, of Old English bearu ‘grove, wood’ (the standard Old English dative bearwe being preserved in Barrow ). In some cases the surname may be topographic in origin, from atte beare ‘at the grove’. Some may be from Middle English bere ‘woodland swine-pasture’ (Old English bǣr). Compare Bear 1.
English: variant of   Bear   2 ‘bear’.

nameMeanings.name: Gwenllian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: from gwen, the feminine form of gwyn ‘white, fair; blessed, holy’ ( see Gwen ) + lliant ‘flood, flow’ (probably in the transferred sense ‘foamy’ or ‘white’, referring to a pale complexion).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Sibil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Sybil .  Even in classical times there was confusion between the two vowels in this word.

nameMeanings.name: Delahue
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived beside a road, track, or pathway, Old French rue (see Rue ), with fused preposition de and definite article la ‘from the’; or a habitational name for someone from La Rue, the name of several places in various parts of France, of the same origin. Compare Larue and also Lareau .

variants.name: Rue
meanings: Mainly Australian: probably originally a short form of Rachel , but now generally taken as a feminine form of Ray or Raymond , or simply a derivative of ray meaning ‘sunbeam’. In some cases it may be a transferred use of the Scottish surname Rae, originally either a short form of MacRae (from a Gaelic personal name meaning ‘son of grace’) or a nickname from the roebuck. It is often used in combinations such as Rae Ellen and Mary Rae.

en.lifeSummary: When Gwenllian Elizabeth Sibil Delahue de la Bere was born about 1402, in Gwyr, Glamorgan, Wales, her father, John de la Bere IV, was 19 and her mother, Joanna Barre, was 30. She married Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1450, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Ki: Newport, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gwenllian Elizabeth Sibil Delahue de la Bere est née environ 1402, à Gwyr, Glamorgan, Galles, son père, John de la Bere IV, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Joanna Barre, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1450, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

Sie hat Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1450 im Alter von 49 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

Lei sposato Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1450, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Gwenllian Elizabeth Sibil Delahue de la Bere(이)가 대략 1402년 웨일스 글램르건 그w이르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de la Bere IV(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanna Barre(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1450년 영국 잉글랜드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gwenllian Elizabeth Sibil Delahue de la Bere родилась около 1402 г. в местности гвир, глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени John de la Bere IV было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Joanna Barre, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gwilym William de Cameis Camoys Kemeys. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1450 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.
